Plaintiff Anton Andryeyev'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Craig A. Pinedo, Esq. Pinedo Law, Telephone (415) 693-9155, appearing for Plaintiff David Abramovitz;
Andrea Reyna Murphy, Esq., Phillips Spallas & Angstadt, Telephone (415) 278-9400, appearing for Defendant Farhat Bajjaliya;

ARGUED - Hearing is held. The Court having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

THIRD PARTY ANTON ANDRYEYEV'S Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories, And For Sanctions

GRANTED. Andryeyev's Requests for Admissions (Set 1) and Form Interrogatories (Set 2) were proper. Defendants objected to the RFAs on the grounds that the RFAs were premature and not fully complete in themselves. These objections are overruled. Responding parties must answer discovery fully on the basis of facts presently known to them. Nor did the requests violate the rule that a request must be full and complete in and of itself. "[R]eference to other materials or documents or incorporation by reference of such materials is prohibited where the effect is to undermine the rule of 35." (Clement v. Alegre (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1277, 1290.) Where the request incorporates other material but does not undermine the discovery limits, the request is proper and "[t]o conclude otherwise in these circumstances would require a cumbersome redrafting of questions and potentially multiple rounds of discovery, undermining the [Discovery] Act's aim of clarity and simplicity and returning to the era of papering to death the opposition." (Id. [internal quotation marks and brackets omitted].) Defendants objected in their opposition papers to the RFAs on the grounds of work product but that objection was not asserted in the original responses and in any event Defendants may properly be asked to admit or deny facts without invading attorney work product. With respect to the Form Interrogatories, Defendants' objections to FI 17.1 are based on its objections to the RFAs; those objections are overruled. Defendants shall provide code-compliant responses and verifications to RFAs Set 1 and FIs Set 2 within 10 days of the date notice of entry of this order is served on Defendants. Defendants' objections were without substantial justification. Defendants shall pay sanctions in the amount of $3000 to Plaintiffs' counsel. Defendants and their counsel are jointly and severally liable.

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/CVA)
